Review: Aditya Dhar delivers a gripping and ambitious crime-thriller with Dhurandhar: The Revenge. From the very first scene, the film pulls you into a dangerous world of espionage, loyalty, betrayal, and survival. The story follows Jaskirat Singh Rangi as he fully embraces his identity as Hamza Ali Mazari and rises through Karachi's criminal underworld, creating a tense and emotionally charged journey. Ranveer Singh is absolutely phenomenal in the lead role. His transformation is intense, convincing, and filled with emotional depth. He brings both vulnerability and ruthless determination to the character, making Hamza Ali Mazari one of the most memorable protagonists in recent Indian cinema. Industry veterans have even praised his performance as award-worthy. The supporting cast is equally impressive. Akshaye Khanna delivers a powerful and nuanced performance, while Sanjay Dutt commands every scene with his presence. The ensemble cast elevates the narrative and keeps the tension high throughout the film. What truly stands out is Aditya Dhar's direction. The world-building is rich, the action sequences are impactful, and the attention to detail creates an immersive experience. The film balances large-scale action with personal drama, ensuring that the emotional stakes remain just as important as the spectacle. Many viewers have praised the realism of the setting and the film's ability to keep audiences engaged from beginning to end. The cinematography, background score, and production values are top-notch, giving the film an international feel while staying rooted in its story. Despite its ambitious scope, the narrative remains focused on the human cost of power, revenge, and survival. Overall, Dhurandhar: The Revenge is a thrilling cinematic experience that combines strong performances, stylish direction, and an engaging storyline. A must-watch for fans of crime dramas, espionage thrillers, and powerful character-driven cinema..

After the success of the first film, I expected Dhurandhar 2 to raise the stakes and deliver a gripping continuation of Jaskirat Singh Rangi's journey into Karachi's criminal underworld. Unfortunately, the film ends up being a bloated and uneven experience that struggles to justify its lengthy runtime. The biggest issue is the screenplay. Despite an interesting premise involving undercover operations, gang rivalries, loyalty, and betrayal, the narrative moves at a painfully slow pace. Large portions of the film feel repetitive, with the same themes and conflicts being revisited without adding anything meaningful to the story. What should have been a tense crime-thriller often turns into a drawn-out exercise in patience. Ranveer Singh puts in effort as Hamza Ali Mazari, but the character is written almost invincible, leaving little room for suspense or emotional investment. The film repeatedly tells us how dangerous and intelligent he is instead of showing genuine challenges that test him. As a result, many key moments lack impact. Some viewers have similarly noted that the protagonist faces very few meaningful setbacks, making the story predictable. The supporting cast, including Akshaye Khanna and Sanjay Dutt, delivers respectable performances, but most characters are underutilized. Several talented actors appear capable of adding depth to the story, yet they are reduced to serving the plot rather than becoming memorable individuals. According to the film's cast listings, the movie boasts a strong ensemble, but the script rarely gives them enough material to shine. The action sequences are stylish and technically competent, but the excessive focus on violence and spectacle often comes at the expense of storytelling. Emotional scenes feel rushed, while action scenes are stretched far beyond what is necessary. The film seems more interested in looking intense than creating genuine tension. Another major problem is the lack of surprises. Twists are easy to predict, character motivations become obvious early on, and the climax fails to provide a satisfying payoff. Even when major events occur, they rarely carry the emotional weight they should. Director Aditya Dhar clearly has ambition, but Dhurandhar 2 feels like a project that needed tighter editing and a stronger script. What could have been an engaging crime-thriller becomes an overlong film that mistakes scale for depth.

Dhurandhar 2 is an intense, gripping, and brilliantly executed crime-thriller that keeps you invested from start to finish. Director Aditya Dhar delivers a larger-than-life cinematic experience while maintaining strong emotional depth and character development. The story follows Jaskirat Singh Rangi as he dives deeper into his identity as Hamza Ali Mazari, navigating loyalty, betrayal, and survival in a ruthless criminal underworld. Ranveer Singh is simply phenomenal. His transformation is powerful, and he completely owns every scene with a performance full of intensity, vulnerability, and charisma. Akshaye Khanna brings his trademark screen presence and sophistication, while Sanjay Dutt adds tremendous weight and authority to the narrative. The supporting cast is equally impressive, making the world of the film feel authentic and immersive. The film excels in its production values, cinematography, action sequences, and background score. The atmosphere of Karachi's underworld is recreated with remarkable attention to detail, creating a realistic and engaging viewing experience. The screenplay steadily builds tension, leading to several memorable moments that leave a lasting impact. What makes Dhurandhar 2 stand out is its balance between high-stakes action and emotional storytelling. It's not just a crime saga; it's a story about identity, ambition, sacrifice, and survival. Fans of action thrillers, espionage dramas, and character-driven narratives will find plenty to admire here. A must-watch for anyone looking for a powerful, ambitious, and unforgettable cinematic experience.

After all the hype surrounding Dhurandhar 2, I expected a gripping espionage thriller packed with tension, smart writing, and memorable character arcs. Unfortunately, what I got was an overlong, self-indulgent crime saga that mistakes loud action and dark visuals for meaningful storytelling. The premise of Jaskirat Singh Rangi descending deeper into the criminal underworld as Hamza Ali Mazari had tremendous potential. However, the screenplay spends far too much time glorifying violence and gangster politics while neglecting emotional depth and character development. The narrative often feels repetitive, moving from one brutal sequence to another without providing enough substance to justify its lengthy runtime. The film reportedly stretches the story across multiple parts, and it certainly feels bloated. Ranveer Singh delivers a committed performance, but even his dedication cannot save a character that is inconsistently written. The supporting cast, including Akshaye Khanna and Sanjay Dutt, have moments of brilliance, yet many of their scenes feel underutilized or designed purely to create mass" moments rather than advance the story. Director Aditya Dhar clearly aims for an epic crime-thriller experience, but the film often prioritizes style over coherence. Several twists are predictable, the pacing drags in the middle portions, and the emotional stakes never become compelling enough to make viewers truly invest in the characters' journeys. Visually, the film looks polished, and some action sequences are impressive. However, good cinematography alone cannot compensate for a screenplay that struggles to maintain momentum for over three hours. A number of viewers have also criticized the excessive runtime and slow narration, which make the experience feel exhausting rather than engaging. Overall, Dhurandhar 2 is an ambitious film that gets lost in its own scale. Despite a talented cast and a promising premise, it ultimately feels more like a collection of stylish scenes than a tightly written thriller."

Dhurandhar is a bold, intense, and thoroughly engaging crime thriller that keeps you invested from start to finish. Director Aditya Dhar crafts a dark and immersive world where loyalty, betrayal, ambition, and survival collide in spectacular fashion. The story of Jaskirat Singh Rangi's transformation into Hamza Ali Mazari and his rise through Karachi's ruthless criminal underworld is both compelling and emotionally charged. The film's premise revolves around an undercover operative navigating Pakistan's criminal networks while pursuing a dangerous mission. Ranveer Singh delivers one of the strongest performances of his career. His transformation is remarkable, bringing both vulnerability and ferocity to the character. Akshaye Khanna is exceptional as always, commanding every scene with his presence, while Sanjay Dutt adds tremendous gravitas and intensity. The supporting cast also contributes significantly to the film's immersive atmosphere. The cinematography, background score, and production design create a realistic and gritty environment that perfectly complements the story. The action sequences are impactful, but what truly stands out is the emotional depth behind the characters and their choices. Aditya Dhar deserves credit for delivering a large-scale thriller that balances action, suspense, and character development. The film's ambitious storytelling and powerful performances make it a memorable cinematic experience. It's easy to see why many viewers are praising the film, and industry figures have even described it as a game-changing project for Indian cinema. If you're a fan of crime dramas, espionage thrillers, and character-driven storytelling, Dhurandhar is absolutely worth watching. A gripping, high-stakes journey that leaves a lasting impression."
